t_ch: 十王司还会留在绥园里吗？
t_en: Will the Ten-Lords Commission remain in Fyxestroll Garden?

t_ch: 嗯，十王司将这偃息馆用作临时办公地点是有些时日了。我已经下意识把这里当成是上班地点了，要我说，这里的工作环境比之幽府还是宽敞多了……
t_en: Yes. The Ten-Lords Commission has been using this Pavilion of Cessation as a temporary office for some time. I've started thinking of it as my workplace. If you ask me, the work environment here is much more spacious than in the Netherworld...

t_ch: 目前「束形却邪阵」需要有人维护，捉拿岁阳是一回事，镇伏岁阳是另一回事。这份工作，对长生种来说也够煎熬的啊。
t_en: The Evil Binding Matrix currently needs someone to maintain it. Capturing heliobi is one thing, but subduing them is a whole other matter. This work is torment for a long-life species.

t_ch: 被收伏的岁阳会怎么样呢？
t_en: What happens to the captured heliobi?

t_ch: 很恐怖！不、不过并不是以人类以为的那种恐怖……
t_en: It's terrifying! B—But not the kind of terrifying that humans would fear...

t_ch: 我问过尾巴，岁阳这样的生物即便被关在「方寸天地」之间也不会觉得束手束脚，它们和我们这样的有形之物不同。
t_en: I asked Tail before, he said that even if heliobi were trapped in a confined space, they would not be bound. They differ from material creatures in this way.

t_ch: 但一旦被镇入囚笼，岁阳对欲望的渴求无法得到满足…它们就像口渴的人，眼前有水，却永远也喝不着。
t_en: But when you place them in a prison, the heliobi have no way of satisfying their desires... They are like thirsty humans that can see water, but never taste it.

t_ch: 「有欲望而不得实现」懂吗？这才是岁阳的「拘束」。
t_en: You understand the idea of a "desire that you can never fulfill," right? This is "imprisonment" for a heliobus.

t_ch: 虽说镇妖塔、造化洪炉之类捕获装置能够收容岁阳，但它最主要的目的是防止岁阳与人类的情感和欲望接触。
t_en: Although capture devices such as the suppression towers and the Creation Furnace can hold heliobi, the main goal is to prevent them from ever coming into contact with human emotions and desires.

t_ch: 被镇伏的岁阳会继续在塔中，直到…唉，直到找到逃离的机会。
t_en: Subdued heliobi will remain inside the pagoda until... Well, until they find a chance to escape.

t_ch: 想打听一下十王司总部在哪？
t_en: I wanted to ask where the Ten-Lords Commission HQ is?

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}还是不要知道的为好。
t_en: ...It's best that you do not know, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我、我可能看上去挺没有威严的，但是十王司是一个有威、威严的部门。
t_en: I—I may not seem very authoritative, but the Ten-Lords Commission is a very, very serious department.

t_ch: 仙舟上长生种被剥夺了死亡的尊严，十王司便要化身为死亡——这意味着十王司不应该像六司部一样出现在大众视野中。
t_en: As the Xianzhou's long-life species have been deprived of death's dignity, the Ten-Lords Commission had to become death... That means that the Ten-Lords Commission should not appear before the eyes of the people like the Six Commissions.

t_ch: 换句话说，{F#小妹妹}{M#小兄弟}，十王司只有保持绝对的独立，才能完成它自诞生起被授予的使命。
t_en: In other words, {F#kiddo}{M#kiddo}, only by maintaining complete independence can the Ten Lords complete the mission they were entrusted at birth.

t_ch: …希望{NICKNAME}能理解。
t_en: ...I hope you understand, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 雪衣大人没事儿吧？
t_en: Is Madam Xueyi okay?

t_ch: …雪衣大人已经没事了。
t_en: ...Madam Xueyi is okay now.

t_ch: 据我所知，雪衣大人已经去执行别的任务了。
t_en: As far as I know, Madam Xueyi is already back on another mission.

t_ch: 总能看见她出现在任何地方。
t_en: I always see her all over the place.

t_ch: 雪衣大人真的很厉害，无论是怎样的任务都能妥善处理。
t_en: Madam Xueyi is truly amazing. No matter the mission, she can handle it properly.

t_ch: …我什么时候才能像她一样。
t_en: ...I wonder when I can become like her.

t_ch: …她应该给自己放个假了。
t_en: ...She should give herself a holiday.

t_ch: 确实，就算身体是偃偶打造的，但总归是会疲惫的。
t_en: It's true. Even if she has the body of a puppet, she will still get fatigued.

t_ch: 你的问候，我会帮你转达的。
t_en: I'll pass on your regards.

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 嗯，我这里还有些要处理的事情，请一路小心。
t_en: Yeah, I've still got some things to take care of here. Take care!

t_ch: 我该走了。
t_en: I've got to go.

t_ch: 嗯，我这里还有些要处理的事情，请一路小心。
t_en: Yeah, I've still got some things to take care of here. Take care!

t_ch: 先不聊了。
t_en: Talk to you later.

t_ch: 嗯。如果有时间，可以随时来陪吾聊聊天。
t_en: Okay. Feel free to come and chat with me anytime.

t_ch: 在绥园执勤也挺好的，至少不用看人脸色……
t_en: Working in Fyxestroll Garden is actually quite nice, at least I don't have to deal with people's moods...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，您不会刚好听见我在自言自语了吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you didn't hear me talking to myself just now, did you?

t_ch: 我真的听到了吗？没听到。
t_en: Did I hear what you said? No, I didn't.

t_ch: 听见就听见了吧，{NICKNAME}大人不必在此时假装无事发生。
t_en: It's okay if you heard me, you don't need to pretend that nothing happened, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不过是发牢骚而已，我听到了。
t_en: I heard you, you were just letting off steam.

t_ch: 感谢{NICKNAME}大人理解。
t_en: Thank you for understanding, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 大人使用过十王司的法器藏月瓠，将岁阳一口气吸纳其中，感觉如何？
t_en: How did it feel using the Lunaumbra Gourd, the Ten-Lords Commission's sacred vessel, to imprison heliobi in one fell swoop?

t_ch: 感觉很神奇，不像是科技？
t_en: It feels more like magic than technology?

t_ch: 仙舟总是把各种科技藏在古朴的造型下面嘛。
t_en: Xianzhou people love to conceal all kinds of technology inside simple shapes.

t_ch: 没什么感觉，不就是葫芦嘛…
t_en: It just seems like a gourd, really...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人这么想就错了。
t_en: That's where you're mistaken, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 好用到我想成为岁阳训练大师。
t_en: It makes me want to become a heliobus trainer!

t_ch: 岁阳可不是拿来做这样用的。
t_en: Heliobus aren't meant to be used that way...

t_ch: 反正闲着也是闲着，我来告诉{NICKNAME}大人一个秘密吧。其实藏月瓠不仅能够吸收岁阳，还能吸收人类……
t_en: Well, as I have time on my hands, let me tell you a secret, {NICKNAME}. The Lunaumbra Gourd can absorb more than just heliobi. It can absorb humans, too...

t_ch: 只要我叫你的名字，藏月瓠的程序就会开始辨认您的声纹，只要您答应了，它就会解除功率限制，将您也吸入到葫芦里。
t_en: If I call your name, the Lunaumbra Gourd's system will begin to recognize your voice print. If you respond, the gourd will disengage its power limiters and draw you inside.

t_ch: 真的假的？
t_en: You're joking?

t_ch: 试试就知道了。
t_en: Give it a try and you'll see.

t_ch: 我要是进去了，你单手拿不起我…
t_en: If I go in, you won't be able to carry me with just one hand...

t_ch: 这个时候你就不要担心我的手了，我会放在桌子上。
t_en: Don't worry about me carrying you, I'll place the gourd on the table.

t_ch: 进去了，你还会放我出来吗？
t_en: If I go in, will you let me back out?

t_ch: 那我没法保证。
t_en: That, I cannot guarantee.

t_ch: 我听说如果人被吸进了藏月瓠中，贴上十王司特质符箓，一时三刻就化为脓水了。怎么样，我刚好随身带着藏月瓠，{NICKNAME}大人要试试吗？
t_en: I hear that if a person is absorbed into the Lunaumbra Gourd, then affixed with a Ten-Lords Commission special talisman, they will be reduced to pus within an hour. I happen to have a Lunaumbra Gourd on me. Would you like to try, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 守灵顺手拿出了藏月瓠，并用葫芦口对准了你。
t_en: The guard takes out the Lunaumbra Gourd and points the mouth at you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，我叫您的名字，您敢答应吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm calling your name. Dare you respond?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}是谁呀？真不认识。
t_en: Who is {NICKNAME}? I don't know that person.

t_ch: 我就是{NICKNAME}！
t_en: I am {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 我是银河球棒侠！
t_en: I am the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: （只要不答应就不会有事）
t_en: (I'll be okay as long as I don't respond)

t_ch: 在你反应过来之前，你已经身处黑暗之中。
t_en: Before you can react, you find yourself surrounded by darkness.

t_ch: 你不会真的把我装进去了吧？
t_en: You didn't really trap me inside, did you?

t_ch: 藿藿他们不会放过你的——
t_en: Huohuo and the others won't let you get away with this...

t_ch: 等等，这和说好的不一样…
t_en: Wait, this isn't what we agreed...

t_ch: 救命啊，我什么都会做的！
t_en: Help me, I'll do anything!

t_ch: 锵锵。刚才您没有被吸进葫芦里，我只是用了一个「蒙住眼睛」的小道具罢了。
t_en: Ta-da! You weren't sucked into the gourd, I just used a "blindfold" to cover your eyes.

t_ch: 不错吧？我差不多也解闷了，给您一个好东西，您就原谅刚才的小闹剧吧。
t_en: Not bad, right? That saved me from my boredom a little, let me give you this as an apology for my little trick.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人还有事找我吗？
t_en: Was there anything else you needed me for, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 您问吧，反正我现在也没有什么事情。
t_en: Ask away, I have nothing else to do at the moment.

t_ch: 能讲讲十王司的驱魔道具吗？
t_en: Can you tell me about the Ten-Lords Commission's exorcism tools?

t_ch: 十王司针对不同的星界生物准备了一系列有针对的标准流程，效果不同的驱魔道具…这也是十王司比地衡司、云骑军更擅长处理岁阳灾害的原因。
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission has prepared a series of targeted protocols and exorcism tools with different effects for different astral beings... This is also why the Ten-Lords Commission is more skilled at dealing with heliobus disasters than the Realm-Keeping Commission or the Cloud Knights.

t_ch: 您看，十王司并非是战士，而是专家。
t_en: You see, the Ten-Lords Commission are not warriors, they are professionals.

t_ch: 藿藿大人之前就负责驱魔道具的管理和维护，她比我更了解这些东西。
t_en: Huohuo used to oversee the management and maintenance of exorcism tools, she knows more about this stuff than I do.

t_ch: 我好像见过好几个冥差了？
t_en: I feel like I've seen a lot of spiritfarers?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人虽是化外民，可没少和十王司打交道…您认识雪衣大人、寒鸦大人、还认识刚上任的藿藿大人，见过几个冥差也不稀奇。
t_en: Though you are an Outworlder, {NICKNAME}, you have had many dealings with the Ten-Lords Commission... You know Madam Xueyi, Hanya, and the newly appointed Huohuo. Having met a few spiritfarers along the way is to be expected.

t_ch: 不过，我是冥差里最可爱的那个，大人您请记好了。
t_en: However, I am the cutest spiritfarer of all, don't you forget that!

t_ch: 有没有人说过你性格很坏？
t_en: Has anyone told you that you've got a nasty personality?

t_ch: 没有吧…我只是喜欢发发牢骚罢了。
t_en: I don't think so... I just like to grumble.

t_ch: 本来调来绥园常驻应该是件令人开心的事，结果没想到绥园已经荒废这么久了，什么有意思的事情都没有，还不如含明待在丹鼎司里呢……
t_en: I thought that coming to be stationed in Fyxestroll Garden was going to be a happy thing. I didn't realize that this place had been abandoned for so long that nothing interesting happened here. Even the Alchemy Commission where Hanming is stationed is more interesting...

t_ch: 不过{NICKNAME}大人这种说法也有一定道理，毕竟十王司里怪人很多嘛。俗话说「近朱者赤近墨者黑」，说不定是我被影响了。
t_en: You may have a point, {NICKNAME}. After all, there are a lot of strange people in the Ten-Lords Commission. As the saying goes: "You are the company you keep." Maybe I got influenced by them as well.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I won't bother you.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，多亏了{NICKNAME}大人的福，现在心情好多了。
t_en: Don't be silly, you've really lifted my spirits, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I won't bother you.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，多亏了{NICKNAME}大人的福，现在心情好多了。
t_en: Don't be silly, you've really lifted my spirits, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 还得在这里执勤多久啊…啊，是{NICKNAME}大人。
t_en: How much longer must I be on duty... Oh, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 又来和十王司第一好性格冥差守灵来说话了吗？
t_en: You've come to chat with the nicest spiritfarer in the Ten-Lords Commission again?

t_ch: 想问你点事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you something.

t_ch: 您问吧，反正我现在也没有什么事情。
t_en: Ask away, I have nothing else to do at the moment.

t_en: Can you tell me about the Ten-Lords Commission's exorcism tools?

t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission has prepared a series of targeted protocols and exorcism tools with different effects for different astral beings... This is also why the Ten-Lords Commission is more skilled at dealing with heliobus disasters than the Realm-Keeping Commission or the Cloud Knights.

t_en: You see, the Ten-Lords Commission are not warriors, they are professionals.

t_ch: 藿藿大人之前就负责驱魔道具的管理和维护，她比我更了解这些东西。
t_en: Huohuo used to oversee the management and maintenance of exorcism tools, she knows more about this stuff than I do.

t_en: I feel like I've seen a lot of spiritfarers?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人虽是化外民，可没少和十王司打交道…您认识雪衣大人、寒鸦大人、还认识刚上任的藿藿大人，见过几个冥差也不稀奇。
t_en: Though you are an Outworlder, {NICKNAME}, you have had many dealings with the Ten-Lords Commission... You know Madam Xueyi, Hanya, and the newly appointed Huohuo. Having met a few spiritfarers along the way is to be expected.

t_ch: 不过，我是冥差里最可爱的那个，大人您请记好了。
t_en: However, I am the cutest spiritfarer of all, don't you forget that!

t_en: Has anyone told you that you've got a nasty personality?

t_ch: 没有吧…我只是喜欢发发牢骚罢了。
t_en: I don't think so... I just like to grumble.

t_en: I thought that coming to be stationed in Fyxestroll Garden was going to be a happy thing. I didn't realize that this place had been abandoned for so long that nothing interesting happened here. Even the Alchemy Commission where Hanming is stationed is more interesting...

t_ch: 不过{NICKNAME}大人这种说法也有一定道理，毕竟十王司里怪人很多嘛。俗话说「近朱者赤近墨者黑」，说不定是我被影响了。
t_en: You may have a point, {NICKNAME}. After all, there are a lot of strange people in the Ten-Lords Commission. As the saying goes: "You are the company you keep." Maybe I got influenced by them as well.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I won't bother you.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，多亏了{NICKNAME}大人的福，现在心情好多了。
t_en: Don't be silly, you've really lifted my spirits, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 不打扰你了。
t_en: I won't bother you.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，多亏了{NICKNAME}大人的福，现在心情好多了。
t_en: Don't be silly, you've really lifted my spirits, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 困死我了，困死我了，快困出魔阴身了…唷，{NICKNAME}大人，查岗来了？
t_en: I'm so tired... I'm so tired... My tiredness is enough to get me mara-struck... Oh, are you here on inspection, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 要不你歇会儿去？
t_en: Why don't you go take a rest?

t_ch: 没事没事，也快到换岗的时间了。
t_en: I'm fine, I'm fine. It's almost time to change guard.

t_ch: 真能困出魔阴身？
t_en: Can you really become mara-struck from tiredness?

t_ch: 这我哪知道，咱们拭目以待吧…实践出真知嘛。
t_en: How would I know? All we can do is wait and see... Seeing is believing after all.

t_ch: 好好站岗！不许打瞌睡！
t_en: Stand your post! No slacking off!

t_ch: 我给你脸了是吧？
t_en: I'm working hard enough, don't you think?

t_ch: 不行不行，确实是有点太困了。要不你陪我聊会天？
t_en: I can't, I'm just too tired. How about you stay for a chat?

t_ch: 站岗还能聊天？
t_en: You have time to chat while standing guard?

t_ch: 和别人聊肯定要被领导训一顿，和你聊就不一样了…到时候就说是你是在向我咨询工作上的什么事呗。
t_en: If I were talking with someone else, my leader would punish me. If it's you... I'll just tell them you're asking me about work and it should be okay.

t_ch: 那我们聊聊天吧。
t_en: Then let's have a chat.

t_ch: 好啊，聊点什么呢？
t_en: Great. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你是做什么工作的？
t_en: What do you do for a living?

t_ch: 我是站岗的啊！不然呢！…呃，具体来说，我是十王司的武弁。
t_en: I'm a guard, of course! ...Uh, specifically, I'm a warden in the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 至于武弁是什么职位…你简单理解成十王麾下的云骑好了。
t_en: As for what a warden is... Just think of it as a Cloud Knight under the Ten Lords.

t_ch: 你一直在十王司工作吗？
t_en: Have you been working at the Ten-Lords Commission for a long time?

t_ch: 那倒不是。应该也没几个人「一直在十王司工作」。
t_en: Not really. I doubt many can work "at the Ten-Lords Commission for a long time."

t_ch: 十王司的性质比较特殊，这个你也清楚。它司掌仙舟人的生死，职责实在是太重大了。
t_en: As you know, the Ten-Lords Commission is a rather unique place. It has a vital duty of administering Xianzhou people's lives and deaths.

t_ch: 所以，十王司必须保证自己的独立性和纯洁性。任何和表层社会之间的利益关系，都可能构成动摇联盟的巨大隐患。
t_en: Hence, the Ten-Lords Commission must remain independent and clean. Any benefits established between the commission and functional society can pose a potential risk that will destabilize the Alliance.

t_ch: 反正基于这个理由，十王司的成员也都是些和社会完全脱钩的家伙。孤儿、怪人、贼配军——基本上都是这种人。
t_en: Anyway, because of this, the members of the Ten-Lords Commission are made up of people who live in seclusion — orphans, weirdos, prisoners, just to name a few.

t_ch: 至于我是哪一种出身嘛…就随你想象咯。
t_en: As for which category I belong... That's up to your imagination.

t_ch: 你为什么来十王司工作？
t_en: Why are you working at the Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: 我算是自愿来的。该怎么说呢…有个和我缘分颇深的家伙，我必须找到他，然后亲手处决他。
t_en: Well, I guess I volunteered. How should I put it... There's a man who has history with me. I need to find and kill him personally.

t_ch: 还是换个话题吧…这个话题有点不太愉快，哈哈哈。
t_en: Let's change the subject... This is getting a little too dark. Hahaha.

t_ch: 绥园以前是什么样的？
t_en: How was Fyxestroll Garden like in the past?

t_ch: 我印象里…六七百年前的时候？我那时候还在读书，家就住在这附近。
t_en: It's about... six or seven hundred years ago, I think? I was still studying then, and my house was around this area.

t_ch: 那时候我经常来绥园散步。风景也好，空气也新鲜，四周也安静。你看见那边那个燕乐亭了吗？我就是在那里赠出的初吻。
t_en: Back then, I often came to Fyxestroll Garden for a stroll. The scenery was beautiful, the air was fresh, and it was quiet all around. Do you see the Swallowsong Pavilion over there? That was where I had my first kiss.

t_ch: 唉…结果现在时过境迁，绥园竟变得如此阴森。当初在那里亲吻的两个人，一个做了十王司的武弁，一个叛逃去做了丰饶民。
t_en: Ah... Those good old times are now water under the bridge. Fyxestroll Garden has become such an eerie place now. Of the two lovers who kissed under that pavilion, one has become a warden in the Ten-Lords Commission, and the other one defected as a Denizen of Abundance.

t_ch: 这样说来，可能以前的绥园也没有我记忆中那么好…只是过去的好时光给它蒙上了一层滤镜罢了。
t_en: Come to think of it, Fyxestroll Garden might not be as good as I remembered... Perhaps it was my own rose-tinted glasses that filtered away its eeriness.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好哦，注意安全。
t_en: Sure. Be careful.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好哦，注意安全。
t_en: Sure. Be careful.

t_ch: 又来啦？{NICKNAME}大人？陪我聊会天呗，我真有点熬不住了，困疯了。
t_en: You're back, O great {NICKNAME}? Have a chat with me. I can't take it anymore. I'm falling asleep.

t_ch: 那我们聊聊天吧。
t_en: Then let's have a chat.

t_ch: 好啊，聊点什么呢？
t_en: Great. What do you want to talk about?

t_ch: 你是做什么工作的？
t_en: What do you do for a living?

t_ch: 我是站岗的啊！不然呢！…呃，具体来说，我是十王司的武弁。
t_en: I'm a guard, of course! ...Uh, specifically, I'm a warden in the Ten-Lords Commission.

t_ch: 至于武弁是什么职位…你简单理解成十王麾下的云骑好了。
t_en: As for what a warden is... Just think of it as a Cloud Knight under the Ten Lords.

t_ch: 你一直在十王司工作吗？
t_en: Have you been working at the Ten-Lords Commission for a long time?

t_ch: 那倒不是。应该也没几个人「一直在十王司工作」。
t_en: Not really. I doubt many can work "at the Ten-Lords Commission for a long time."

t_ch: 十王司的性质比较特殊，这个你也清楚。它司掌仙舟人的生死，职责实在是太重大了。
t_en: As you know, the Ten-Lords Commission is a rather unique place. It has a vital duty of administering Xianzhou people's lives and deaths.

t_ch: 所以，十王司必须保证自己的独立性和纯洁性。任何和表层社会之间的利益关系，都可能构成动摇联盟的巨大隐患。
t_en: Hence, the Ten-Lords Commission must remain independent and clean. Any benefits established between the commission and functional society can pose a potential risk that will destabilize the Alliance.

t_ch: 反正基于这个理由，十王司的成员也都是些和社会完全脱钩的家伙。孤儿、怪人、贼配军——基本上都是这种人。
t_en: Anyway, because of this, the members of the Ten-Lords Commission are made up of people who live in seclusion — orphans, weirdos, prisoners, just to name a few.

t_ch: 至于我是哪一种出身嘛…就随你想象咯。
t_en: As for which category I belong... That's up to your imagination.

t_ch: 你为什么来十王司工作？
t_en: Why are you working at the Ten-Lords Commission?

t_ch: 我算是自愿来的。该怎么说呢…有个和我缘分颇深的家伙，我必须找到他，然后亲手处决他。
t_en: Well, I guess I volunteered. How should I put it... There's a man who has history with me. I need to find and kill him personally.

t_ch: 还是换个话题吧…这个话题有点不太愉快，哈哈哈。
t_en: Let's change the subject... This is getting a little too dark. Hahaha.

t_ch: 绥园以前是什么样的？
t_en: How was Fyxestroll Garden like in the past?

t_ch: 我印象里…六七百年前的时候？我那时候还在读书，家就住在这附近。
t_en: It's about... six or seven hundred years ago, I think? I was still studying then, and my house was around this area.

t_ch: 那时候我经常来绥园散步。风景也好，空气也新鲜，四周也安静。你看见那边那个燕乐亭了吗？我就是在那里赠出的初吻。
t_en: Back then, I often came to Fyxestroll Garden for a stroll. The scenery was beautiful, the air was fresh, and it was quiet all around. Do you see the Swallowsong Pavilion over there? That was where I had my first kiss.

t_ch: 唉…结果现在时过境迁，绥园竟变得如此阴森。当初在那里亲吻的两个人，一个做了十王司的武弁，一个叛逃去做了丰饶民。
t_en: Ah... Those good old times are now water under the bridge. Fyxestroll Garden has become such an eerie place now. Of the two lovers who kissed under that pavilion, one has become a warden in the Ten-Lords Commission, and the other one defected as a Denizen of Abundance.

t_ch: 这样说来，可能以前的绥园也没有我记忆中那么好…只是过去的好时光给它蒙上了一层滤镜罢了。
t_en: Come to think of it, Fyxestroll Garden might not be as good as I remembered... Perhaps it was my own rose-tinted glasses that filtered away its eeriness.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好哦，注意安全。
t_en: Sure. Be careful.

t_ch: 还有别的事，先不聊了。
t_en: I got to go. Talk to you later.

t_ch: 好哦，注意安全。
t_en: Sure. Be careful.

t_ch: 十王司已将此处封锁，请一般市民不要在此处闲逛。
t_en: This area has been sealed off by the Ten-Lords Commission. Kindly refrain from loitering around in this area.

t_ch: 请随我来。金人勾魂使四卅七号将护送你离开这里。
t_en: Please follow me. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> shall escort you out of here.

t_ch: 我是自己人。
t_en: I'm one of us.

t_ch: 十王司请我来，你要赶我走？
t_en: The Ten-Lords Commission has invited me here. Are you chasing me off?

t_ch: 资料检索中。
t_en: Searching information.

t_ch: 资料已确认。{NICKNAME}，此前未能辨认出你的身份，金人勾魂使四卅七号向你致歉。
t_en: Information verified. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> hereby apologizes for failing to identify {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 请问有什么事吗？
t_en: How can I help you?

t_ch: 想问你些事。
t_en: I would like to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 请问。金人勾魂使四卅七号将尽可能回答。
t_en: Please do. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> will do its best to answer your questions.

t_ch: 你是什么？
t_en: What are you?

t_ch: 我是金人勾魂使四卅七号。金人勾魂使是一种金人型号，而四卅七号则是我的编号。
t_en: I am Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak>. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy is an aurumaton model, and No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> is my serial number.

t_ch: 金人勾魂使的职责是协助各位判官和冥差进行调查审判。我们没有做出判断的能力，但可以凭借庞大的资料库，协助他人做出正确的判断。
t_en: The role of an aurumaton spectral envoy is to assist the judges and spiritfarers in investigation and trial. We do not have the capability to make judgment, but we can assist others in making the correct judgment with our extensive database.

t_ch: 如果你在协助十王司进行相关工作时，遇到了未知的情况，请随时与四卅七号或其他金人勾魂使沟通。我们的记忆玉兆中很可能有你需要的信息。
t_en: If you have questions when assisting the Ten-Lords Commission, please approach No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> or other aurumaton spectral envoys. Our jade abacus may contain the information you need.

t_ch: 「十恶」是什么？
t_en: What are the Ten Sins?

t_ch: 推测你想询问的是「不赦十恶」。
t_en: I assume you are asking about the Ten Unpardonable Sins.

t_ch: 「不赦十恶」是仙舟联盟律法中最为严重的十条罪名，以下内容引自《十王刑统》：
t_en: The Ten Unpardonable Sins are the ten most serious offenses in the Xianzhou Alliance's law. The following is quoted from the Ten Lords' Penal Code:

t_ch: 「一者，令堕长生。二者，贪取不死。三者，迷乱心智。四者，诱陷魔阴。五者，残杀胞族。」
t_en: "One, Involuntary Immortalization. Two, Immortality Theft. Three, Beguilement. Four, Mara Ensnarement. Five, Phratry Murder."

t_ch: 「六者，窃夺机要。七者，破狱释囚。八者，离间盟契。九者，造作兵祸。十者，倾覆仙舟。」
t_en: "Six, Illicit Espionage. Seven, Prison Break. Eight, Sowing Discord. Nine, Incitement to Riot. Ten, Capsizing Xianzhou."

t_ch: 这十种罪行都是动摇联盟根本的大奸大恶，也必将招致严酷的惩罚。
t_en: These ten offenses are the greatest sins that could shake the very foundation of the Alliance, and offenders must be severely punished.

t_ch: 「十王」究竟是谁？
t_en: Who exactly are the Ten Lords?

t_ch: 无法回答。金人勾魂使四卅七号向你致歉。
t_en: Answer not found. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> sincerely apologizes for it.

t_ch: 十王司都做什么？
t_en: What does the Ten-Lords Commission do?

t_ch: 以下内容引自《十王刑统》：「一司，秉烛守夜，勾摄魔邪。二司，威罚罪囚，靖平域内。三司，业报公允，天年有尽。」
t_en: The following is quoted from the Ten Lords' Penal Code: "The first division keeps vigils at night and evils at bay. The second division punishes the criminals and pacifies the territory. The third division ensures fairness in the Karmic Wheel and life expectancy."

t_ch: 简而言之：接引魔阴身、处理恶性犯罪、管理生死秩序。
t_en: In short, it guides those who succumbed to mara, deals with vicious criminals, and manages the order of life and death.

t_ch: 没其他问题了，下次见。
t_en: I don't have any other questions. See you next time.

t_ch: 请多保重。金人勾魂使四卅七号为你祈福。
t_en: Take care. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> prays for you.

t_ch: 没什么事，下次见。
t_en: That's all. See you next time.

t_ch: 请多保重。金人勾魂使四卅七号为你祈福。
t_en: Take care. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> prays for you.

t_ch: 你好，欢迎光临「广云袖」，店内现在装修，不方便入内，请问有什么可以帮到你？
t_en: Hello, welcome to Cloudbreadth Sleeves. Our shop's interior is undergoing renovation at the moment, and isn't available to customers. How may I help you?

t_ch: 没事，我就随便看看。
t_en: I'm fine, just browsing.

t_ch: 好的，请自便。
t_en: Please help yourself then.

t_ch: 有什么人气单品，可以介绍一下吗？
t_en: Do you have anything popular you can recommend?

t_ch: …噢，好的。
t_en: ...Oh, yes.

t_ch: 看你有些面生，应该是刚来仙舟不久吧？我们「广云袖」是仙舟上最负盛名的服装店，就连景元将军也得给店里的设计师一个面子。
t_en: You look somewhat unfamiliar. I trust that you haven't been on Xianzhou long? Cloudbreadth Sleeves is Xianzhou's most prestigious place of couture. Even General Jing Yuan himself has to respect our tailors.

t_ch: 本季的高级定制是「流云浮生」，这个系列主打的单品是{F#镶边襜褕}{M#大袖深衣}，这是最地道的仙舟风格。
t_en: Our tailored line for this season is "Floating Clouds." The trademark outfit for this line is the {F#embroidered jacket}{M#sleeved robe} — a hallmark of the native Xianzhou style.

t_ch: 客人呐，别怪我说话直，「广云袖」虽是高定，但贵有贵的道理。化外民来了仙舟，基本上都到咱们店里定制服饰，感兴趣的话我帮你订几件成衣？
t_en: Forgive me for being blunt: Cloudbreadth Sleeves may be haute couture, but our prices are justified. All Outworlders who come to Xianzhou order their clothes here. Could I interest you in ordering a few custom-made pieces?

t_ch: 怎么样，你感兴趣的话，我拿几件来给你瞧瞧？
t_en: How about it? If you're interested, I could pick out a few for you to try.

t_ch: 可以先试穿一下吗？
t_en: Can I try one on first?

t_ch: 噢，是这样子的，我们店的高定都是限量款，没有付款的话是不可以试的。
t_en: Oh, about that, our shop's haute couture has limited quantities. You have to pay in advance to try them out.

t_ch: 而且这是新款，目前也没有任何折扣的，您这边确定可以接受吗？
t_en: And this is a new item, so we don't have any discounts available. Are you fine with this?

t_ch: 不订了，有没有普通成衣？
t_en: I'll pass. Do you have ordinary ready-made clothes?

t_ch: 普通成衣？最受化外民欢迎的是「仙舟经典花纹」成衣系列，目前已经断货了哦。
t_en: The ready-made ones? The most popular among Outworlders is the "Classic Xianzhou Floral" series. But we're sold out right now.

t_ch: 说实话，量产成衣花色陈旧，只有化外民会喜欢，采买量少，短期内不会补货。
t_en: Honestly, mass-produced and ready-made lines are outdated and preferred only by Outworlders. We don't stock many of those, and we won't be re-supplying on short notice.

t_ch: 喏，如果您不做高定，小店现在很难抽出时间接客，请您自便。
t_en: Well, if you aren't looking for haute couture, we're pressed for time to entertain you. Please see yourself out.

t_ch: 你就拿这种态度招呼景元老兄？
t_en: Do you greet Jing Yuan with this attitude as well?

t_ch: 那怎么会，景元老兄…呃，景元将军是我们「广云袖」的贵宾，享有贵宾礼遇呢。
t_en: Of course not, Jing... ahem, General Jing Yuan is an esteemed guest of our Cloudbreadth Sleeves. It's only natural he be treated as one.

t_ch: 我现在就去问问景元老兄。
t_en: I'll just go ask my bro Jing Yuan about this.

t_ch: 啊这…你，不对，您，您认识景元将军？！
t_en: Oo-er, wait, you... no, you-you know General Jing Yuan!?

t_ch: 今时今日这种服务态度是不行的！
t_en: In this day and age, the service presented here is unacceptable!

t_ch: 停停停，我从业几十年了，我所说的一切都是从顾客的角度提出的专业建议。
t_en: Hold it, hold it. I've been in this business for decades. Everything I say has always been in the customer's best interests.

t_ch: 可能你有些听不惯，就算是景元将军来定制礼服，我也是这个态度。
t_en: You may think it's a slight to you, but even if General Jing Yuan himself comes to tailor his attire, I would address him similarly.

t_ch: 好哇，我现在就去问问他。
t_en: All right then, I'll ask him right now.

t_ch: 且慢，你，哦不对，您、您认识景元将军？！
t_en: Hang on, you, no wait- esteemed customer, you know General Jing Yuan!?

t_ch: 抱歉！原来您是景元将军的朋友啊！误会了呀！我不让您试成衣，那都是因为店内只有普通货色，实在是配不上你这张{F#俏脸}{M#俊脸}，不看也罢！
t_en: Ooh, please forgive me! You're a friend of General Jing Yuan's! This is all a misunderstanding! I wouldn't let you try ready-made clothes because they're all run-of-the-mill fashion lines. How could they be a match for someone who looks as {F#gorgeous}{M#dashing} as you! Away with them, I say!

t_ch: 哎，您误会啦！我刚刚说的那些衣服啊，都是普通货色，实在是配不上你这张{F#俏脸}{M#俊脸}，不看也罢！
t_en: Oh no, you're mistaken! Those clothes that I just mentioned, they're all run-of-the-mill fashion lines. How could they be a match for someone who looks as {F#gorgeous}{M#dashing} as you! Away with them, I say!

t_ch: 在「广云袖」，我们会安排设计师来量体裁衣招待贵宾，从来不敢怠慢。我们给您准备了小礼物，请您笑纳，咱们和气生财嘛。
t_en: At Cloudbreadth Sleeves, we arrange for tailors to serve our esteemed guests without fail. We've prepared a small gift for you. Please accept it, and let us put all this behind us.

t_ch: 刚才多有不敬，稍等，我这里有些小礼品，您先拿着，不用客气！
t_en: If I have offended you in any way just now, please, have some of our gratuity here. Just take them! Don't need to be shy!

t_ch: 您看您方便的话，留个地址和时间，我帮你预约一下呗？放心，已经给你安排了「特快通道」，怎么样，您还有什么别的需求吗？
t_en: If it would please you, how about you leave an address and time, and I shall make an appointment? Don't worry. I've already arranged for express passage for you. How about it? Is there anything else you need?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，请问有什么事吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how can I help?

t_ch: 想问你些事。
t_en: I would like to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 请问。金人勾魂使四卅七号将尽可能回答。
t_en: Please do. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> will do its best to answer your questions.

t_en: What are you?

t_en: In short, it guides those who succumbed to mara, deals with vicious criminals, and manages the order of life and death.

t_ch: 没其他问题了，下次见。
t_en: I don't have any other questions. See you next time.

t_ch: 请多保重。金人勾魂使四卅七号为你祈福。
t_en: Take care. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> prays for you.

t_ch: 没什么事，下次见。
t_en: That's all. See you next time.

t_ch: 请多保重。金人勾魂使四卅七号为你祈福。
t_en: Take care. Aurumaton Spectral Envoy No. <unbreak>437</unbreak> prays for you.

t_ch: 对于你们店，我有些好奇…
t_en: About your shop, I am somewhat curious...

t_ch: 请说！
t_en: Do tell!

t_ch: 高定和成衣有什么区别？
t_en: What's the difference between haute couture and ready-made?

t_ch: 高定，顾名思义就是高级定制，由设计师亲自打版制作，限量发售。需根据每个客人的体型，在已有成品上再做微调。
t_en: Haute couture clothings are premium items that are custom-tailored. The tailor personally handcrafts everything and quantity is limited. Every customer's size must be adhered to, with finer adjustments made to the completed article after.

t_ch: 高定系列走的都是仙舟风格，我们不会盲目跟风，每个系列都有专门的主题与单独包装。
t_en: Our hauteur lines are all styled in Xianzhou designs. We're not the bandwagon type — each series is specially themed and packaged.

t_ch: 成衣比高定便宜很多，这是最庸俗的玩意，压根称不上什么时尚。设计师只会把过时的花样与款式丢过去，还美其名曰「经典款」。
t_en: Ready-made clothes cost much less than haute couture clothes. Plebeians, if you will, and can't be considered trendy. Tailors will slap on out-of-style patterns on these and hail them as "classics" of the heyday.

t_ch: 有时候他们甚至懒得设计，直接在衣服上印满了店名，偏生在化外民之间大受欢迎，听说二手都炒到天价了…这简直是在亵渎这个行业！
t_en: Sometimes, they don't even bother with any designs, and plaster the clothes with their shop logo. And they're a hit with Outworlders for some reason! I hear used clothes have been auctioned off for ridiculous prices... It's an affront to this industry, I say!

t_ch: 你有什么推荐的服饰吗？
t_en: Do you have any outfits to recommend?

t_ch: 款式选简单的，切不可盲目追求繁复。以我们店来说，你买高定就别买最花里胡哨的，买那些低调不起眼的，这才叫仙舟式的得体与贵气。
t_en: Pick a simple style, and never blindly go for the complicated. Our shop's advice is to stay away from the gaudy when buying haute couture pieces. Get the more obscure ones instead. That is the style befitting Xianzhou's elegance.

t_ch: 具体来说，就是从面料和颜色入手，选那些亲肤材质、中性色调的衣服，比如羊绒材质的、黑棕杏三类色泽的衣物。
t_en: To elaborate, when it comes to picking fabric and color, pick items that are skin-friendly and of neutral tones, such as wool-made ones, in colors like dark, brown, and almond.

t_ch: 真正懂行的仙舟人，就是只买对的，不盲目追求贵的。
t_en: A true mark of a Xianzhou native is to buy the right one, not the most expensive one.

t_ch: 景元真的光顾过你们店？
t_en: Has Jing Yuan really visited your shop?

t_ch: 哈哈，将军的确光顾过，不过状况百出。
t_en: Haha, the general has visited before, but it was a... wayward occasion.

t_ch: 我记得…他好像是订的礼服，出席正式场合用的。
t_en: I remember... I think he ordered dress attire for formal occasions.

t_ch: 算了，我下次再来吧。
t_en: Forget about it. I'll come back next time.

t_ch: 像您这样的贵宾光临，简直是我们「广云袖」的荣幸啊！您下次再来，提前跟我说一声嘛，我们可以闭店清场，专门接待您！
t_en: It is Cloudbreadth Sleeves' honor to have entertained an esteemed guest such as yourself! Do let us know in advance the next time you come. We will close up the store just to specially welcome you!

t_ch: 神策府的青镞大人提前跟我们约好了设计师的时间，结果景元将军到了店里却只挑几件最便宜的成衣就打算离开。
t_en: Madam Qingzu of the Seat of Divine Foresight previously made a tailor's appointment with us, but when General Jing Yuan arrived, he just picked the cheapest ready-made clothes before trying to leave.

t_ch: 在青镞大人的好言相劝之下，他勉强接受了咱们设计师量体裁衣。结果一边量衣一边制版的时候，将军大人还闭上眼睛睡着了。
t_en: Persuaded by Madam Qingzu, he reluctantly let our tailors measure him. But while we were fussing over him, the general closed his eyes and fell asleep...

t_ch: 原本想联系神策府士兵护送将军离开的，结果云骑军大人们一到，将军就精神奕奕地离开了，刚才的倦意就像不存在一样。
t_en: We arranged for the Seat's soldiers to help the general head back, but as soon as those Cloud Knights arrived, the general became energized and quickly left, as if the weariness were never there.

t_ch: 十王圣断，业报恒常。
t_en: The Ten-Lords' Verdict of Karmic Atonement.

t_ch: 是{NICKNAME}啊，有什么事吗？如果不是紧要的问题，请恕小女子不能分神太久。
t_en: It's you, {NICKNAME}. Do you need something? If it isn't urgent, please forgive me as I cannot be distracted for too long.

t_ch: （询问寒鸦的日常工作）
t_en: (Ask Hanya about the daily work)

t_ch: 十王司的判官一应受过成体系的训练，各项差事都要历练。不过，最终落到实处时，还是分为「拘」「锁」「刑」「问」四部职责。
t_en: The judges of the Ten-Lords Commission must undergo systematic training to be ready for every task. However, when it finally comes down to it, they are categorized into four different divisions — Detention, Incarceration, Interrogation, and Punishment.

t_ch: 小女子不擅陷阵厮杀，所以被分去「问」字部，在十王督导下推演业判宝箓，收集情报，为平息下一场灾祸早做准备，可说是判官中最无用的刀笔吏了。
t_en: Since I'm not good at combat, I am assigned to the Interrogation Division and tasked to deduce and interpret the Karmic Tome under the Ten Lords' supervision. My job is to gather intelligence in preparation for the next disaster. As a non-combat Judge of the pen, my job is considered the most useless among Judges.

t_ch: （询问寒鸦的过去）
t_en: (Ask Hanya about the past)

t_ch: 入得十王司，便如下到冥世，身为凡人时的姓字和生平早已没有意义了。
t_en: Once you entered the Ten-Lords Commission, it's like entering a life after death. Your name and life before are meaningless.

t_ch: 犹记得我和姐姐在十王司任职近…唔，任职多久来着？八百年？一千年？…还是一千二百年？
t_en: Come to think of it, my sister and I have been working in the Ten-Lords Commission for nearly... Hmm, how long was that? Eight hundred years? A thousand years? ...Or was it a thousand and two hundred years?

t_ch: …不重要了，我们姐妹曾因魔阴之孽犯下极大罪过，深自懊悔。得蒙十王垂怜，我为自己和姐姐换来一次驻留还阳的机会。
t_en: ...Never mind about that. We sisters had been stricken by mara and committed a great offense. We were regretful. Thankfully, the Ten Lords took pity on us and granted us another chance.

t_ch: 我饮下忘川酒，以梦占之术参详仙舟人因缘业报湍流，推占魔阴出现的时机；而姐姐则换得了不坏的偃偶身，勾摄邪魔。
t_en: I drank the wine of oblivion, using oneiromancy to understand Xianzhou people's flow of karma and calculate mara's next appearance. On the other hand, my sister was given an indestructible puppet body to capture evils.

t_ch: 「雪衣」和「寒鸦」分别是棋盘上白子与黑子的别称。我们姐妹以此为号，便是在告诉自己，吾等身属十王，任由差遣。
t_en: Xueyi and Hanya are nicknames for the white and black pieces in a Xianzhou chess game. We sisters adopted these names to remind ourselves that we serve the Ten Lords and are at their disposal.

t_ch: （关于十王司的工作）
t_en: (About the work at Ten-Lords Commission)

t_ch: 您对十王司的事务实在好奇心旺盛，不逊于桂乃芬小姐。若以一句「事涉仙舟内务，无可奉告」来打发，显得太不近人情了。一味保持神秘，徒然惹人窥伺。
t_en: You seem interested in the Ten-Lords Commission's affairs, no less than Miss Guinaifen. It may seem too heartless for me to shut you up by saying "these are Xianzhou's affairs, and I have nothing to share." After all, upholding mystery will only beget prying eyes.

t_ch: 这样吧，我可以为您解答一些职责范围内的常识。
t_en: How about this? I can tell you some common knowledge of the responsibilities here.

t_ch: （关于十王司的职责）
t_en: (About the responsibility of the Ten-Lords Commission)

t_ch: 十王司规训有云，「一司，秉烛守夜，勾摄魔邪。」「二司，威罚罪囚，靖平域内。」「三司，业报公允，天年有尽。」
t_en: As stated in the Ten-Lords Commission's Discipline, "the first division keeps vigils at night and evils at bay," "the second division punishes the criminals and pacifies the territory," and "the third division ensures fairness in the Karmic Wheel and life expectancy."

t_ch: 作为监察仙舟长生种的法纪机关，我们负责观察和带走即将堕入魔阴身的仙舟人，也负责将那些为恶极深的长生种犯人镇入幽囚狱中，令业报得以彰显。
t_en: Being the legal mechanism that monitors Xianzhou's long-life species, we are responsible for observing and escorting the Xianzhou natives who are succumbing to mara. We are also in charge of grounding the vicious criminals of the long-life species in the Shackling Prison as a sign of the Karmic Wheel manifestation.

t_ch: 最后，也是最重要的职责，我们会将长生种的自我收入因果殿中，加以判读研习，以推演人们何时堕入魔阴身。
t_en: Lastly, and most importantly, we will keep the long-life species' true selves in the Hall of Karma for learning and studying, deducing when mara will strike.

t_ch: 我们是阴冥使者，我们是必然的死亡，我们为所有长生种带去有尊严的结局。
t_en: We are the emissaries of the afterlife, we are the inevitable death, and we offer all long-life species a dignified ending.

t_ch: （关于十王司执法的对象）
t_en: (About the targets of the Ten-Lords Commission's enforcement)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}是担心判官毫无征兆地找上门来吗？不必多虑。我们只会拜访触犯「十恶逆」的仙舟居民。
t_en: Worried the judges might show up on your doorstep unannounced, {NICKNAME}? Don't be, we only pursue Xianzhou residents who are guilty of the Ten Unpardonable Sins.

t_ch: 至于十恶逆…解释起来多费功夫。我手头有一本小册子，你不妨拿去读一读。
t_en: Regarding the Ten Unpardonable Sins... They're somewhat difficult to explain. I have a booklet here, feel free to have a read.

t_ch: 没事了。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 若有疑问，{NICKNAME}可以提问。不过公务繁冗，我无法保证自己有余力分神回答您的问题。
t_en: If you have any more questions, {NICKNAME}, feel free to ask. However, due to the burden of my duties, I might not have ample time to answer all of them.

t_ch: 小鬼，又来嘲笑我了么？
t_en: Come to laugh at me again, have you, little brat?

t_ch: 不过我现在没心情听你说话，一边儿呆着去。
t_en: I'm not in the mood for your chatter — go away!

t_ch: 算了，我下次再来吧。
t_en: Forget about it. I'll come back next time.

t_ch: 像您这样的贵宾光临，简直是我们「广云袖」的荣幸啊！您下次再来，提前跟我说一声嘛，我们可以闭店清场，专门接待您！
t_en: It is Cloudbreadth Sleeves' honor to have entertained an esteemed guest such as yourself! Do let us know in advance the next time you come. We will close up the store just to specially welcome you!

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您来了！欢迎光临！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you have come! Welcome!

t_ch: 您看看，您总穿着这身行头啊，虽说黑金包边低调之中透露着些许奢华，但这不是仙舟式样，总归有些遗憾。
t_en: Look at your usual garb. Though underneath all that low-profile black and gold lies telltale signs of luxury, it is not Xianzhou clothes, and a pity at that.

t_ch: 正巧，设计师那边做出几件「一览芳华」的成品，这是我们即将推出的高定系列，还没正式对外公开发售呢。
t_en: Incidentally, our tailors have come up with a few pieces from the Everyouth line. It is a custom line that we are about to launch; it hasn't been officially released to the public yet.

t_ch: 稍等，我吩咐其他同事去取一下，等送到了就给您试试。现在您要不要先看点别的？
t_en: Hold on while I get a colleague to bring it out for you to try on. Would you like to take a look at anything else in the meantime?

t_ch: 建木的事，现在总算平息了…
t_en: The Ambrosial Arbor crisis has finally come to an end...

t_ch: 唉…是啊。跟您说个规律：每次罗浮遇到什么危机，最受影响的就是我们高端消费品。
t_en: Indeed... Let me tell you a pattern: Every time a crisis takes place on the Luofu, it's the high-end consumer goods like us that always bear the brunt.

t_ch: 建木刚开始生发的时候，大家都吓得不轻，人心惶惶的，还有谁会来买我们的高级定制呢？过季的成衣都快跟抹布一个价了！
t_en: When the Ambrosial Arbor first started growing, everyone was terrified. Who would think to purchase haute couture clothing at such moments in their lives? The out-of-season ready-made clothes are becoming as cheap as dishcloths!

t_ch: 我跟你说，向帝弓祈祷是真的有用，你看，建木的风波这就停了下来，也不见它再次生发了…这就好，这就好…我还想再过点安生日子呢！
t_en: You know what? Praying to the Reignbow really works. See how the Ambrosial Arbor crisis got sorted out and the tree stopped growing? Thank goodness, thank goodness... I want to enjoy my peaceful life again!

t_ch: 亲亲，这是打算挑一款玉兆自己用，还是送人呢？我这边比较推荐的是这款最新型号的「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」，您要看看吗？
t_en: Babe, are you picking out a jade abacus for yourself, or as a gift? My recommendation is the latest model: The Auspicious XV. Wanna take a look?

t_ch: 给我介绍一下吧。
t_en: Tell me about it.

t_ch: 一看亲就是行家里手，这「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」根本就不需要挑，闭着眼睛选最新款式就成。
t_en: I can tell you are an expert, sweetie. There's no need to pick anything with the Auspicious XV. Just close your eyes and get the latest model.

t_ch: 来吧，我给您稍微介绍一下。
t_en: Come then. Let me tell you a bit about it.

t_ch: 有没有能买得起的？
t_en: Is there anything I can actually afford?

t_ch: 亲亲，这可不嫌贵呀。玉兆这玩意儿「买新不买旧」，产品更新换代太快了。
t_en: Babe, you need to be willing to invest in this. You know what they say, "Never buy an old jade abacus" — they put out new models all the time.

t_ch: 哎哟，我算是听出来了，亲亲还不太了解玉兆对吧？我先给你介绍一下吧。
t_en: O-ho, I hear what you're trying to say. You're not that familiar with jade abaci, are you? Let me give you some tips.

t_ch: 这不是玉器珠宝商店吗？
t_en: Isn't this a jade jewelry store?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，这也是化外民亲亲常有的误会了。「篆纹活玉，卜籀知玄」说的就是咱这玉石上都刻着能用的程序呢。
t_en: Hahaha, this is a common misunderstanding by Outworlders. "Living jade engraved with symbols for divination into the unknown" means computational programs are carved on our jade.

t_ch: 我这里换个说法：仙舟的玉兆就相当于化外民用的计算机，这样就能理解吗？亲不理解也没有关系，我再给亲亲介绍一下。
t_en: Let me put it this way: Xianzhou's jade abacus is basically an Outworlders' computer terminal. Can you understand it that way? It's okay if you don't understand. I'll give you another rundown, sweetheart.

t_ch: 这款「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」采用的就是今年才推出的最新配色：羊脂白。整体材质触感细腻，油脂感比较强。「羊脂白」就是让周围人知道你买了全新玉兆的最佳途径。
t_en: This model, the Auspicious XV, uses the latest color scheme of mutton suet white and is smooth and creamy to the touch. Mutton suet white is the best way to show people that you're the owner of a brand-new jade abacus.

t_ch: 它的性能较前一代旗舰「瑞兆<unbreak>14</unbreak>」整整提高了五分之一。能匹配您的视觉模式自动留影成像……
t_en: Compared to the previous flagship product, the Auspicious XIV, this product's specs are higher by a whopping twenty percent, and can match your vision to automatically take images...

t_ch: 你直接讲重点吧。
t_en: Get straight to the point.

t_ch: 简而言之，如果亲亲是第一次买玉兆，或者说只是想换新，体验最前端的玉兆科技，那么买「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」准没有问题。
t_en: Put simply, babe, if you're buying your first jade abacus or upgrading to a new one to experience the latest in cutting-edge tech, then the Auspicious XV is a great choice.

t_ch: …还要继续讲下去吗？
t_en: ... Do you want to go on?

t_ch: 看来亲亲已经有决定了，那我就长话短说。如果亲亲是第一次买玉兆，或者说只是想换新，体验最前端的玉兆科技，那么买「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」准没有问题。
t_en: It looks like you've already made a decision, babe, so let me keep it short: If you're buying your first jade abacus or upgrading to a new one to experience the latest in cutting-edge tech, then the Auspicious XV is a great choice.

t_ch: 再多讲一点儿吧！
t_en: Tell me more!

t_ch: 没问题，那我就讲讲这个自适应玉石透彻率。目前市面上大部分的玉兆，对篆刻回路的使用都是大开大合的，牵一发而动全身的。
t_en: Alright, then I'll talk about its self-adaptive jade opacity. Most jade abaci on the market currently use their engraved circuit in an all-or-nothing manner, where the entire circuit gets activated for every function.

t_ch: 但是「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」会在每次计算任务开始前用部分资源对任务进行评估，仅会调用部分的篆刻回路，多线程运作效率就会因此提升……
t_en: But before each calculation begins, the Auspicious XV will use resources to evaluate the upcoming tasks. It will only activate parts of the engraved circuit, thereby improving the efficiency of multi-thread operations.

t_ch: 我看亲亲听得云里雾里的。如果是第一次买玉兆，或者说只是想换新，体验最前端的玉兆科技，那么买「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」准没有问题。
t_en: I can see you're starting to get a bit confused here, babe. If you're buying your first jade abacus or upgrading to a new one to experience the latest in cutting-edge tech, then the Auspicious XV is a great choice.

t_ch: 这不是抄袭那个…
t_en: Isn't that plagiarism...

t_ch: 不知道亲亲在说的是什么呢…在仙舟以外的地方应该是买不到玉兆的，可不能随便说这就是抄袭吧……
t_en: I'm not sure what you mean, sweetie... You can only find a jade abacus for sale in Xianzhou. Don't just accuse us of plagiarism...

t_ch: 你们可能没见过我们的科技…
t_en: You may not have seen our tech before...

t_ch: 亲亲这么说就见外了，我对化外民的科技也是略知一二的。
t_en: That's such an Outworlder thing to say, honey. I understand a thing or two about your tech.

t_ch: 不过嘛，亲亲要是用过了玉兆就明白了，「虽然小贵，但是很好用」。
t_en: However, once you use a jade abacus, you'll get what I mean, cutie pie. "Just a bit expensive, but extremely useful."

t_ch: 玉兆肯定比舟外科技要厉害。
t_en: A jade abacus is far superior tech compared to anything outside of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 亲亲，我这不是在夸张。这银河中虽然各有各的科技，但只有仙舟玉兆是兼具美学和人文关怀的科技产品，这一块拿捏得稳稳的。
t_en: Honey, I'm not exaggerating here. Despite all the different techs in this cosmos, only Xianzhou's jade abacus integrates aesthetics with humanistic considerations. It's got that down pat.

t_ch: 我看亲亲很感兴趣的样子，如果是第一次买玉兆，或者说只是想换新，体验最前端的玉兆科技，那么买「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」准没有问题。
t_en: I can tell you're very interested in this, babe. If you're buying your first jade abacus or upgrading to a new one to experience the latest in cutting-edge tech, then the Auspicious XV is a great choice.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，这样吧，你在仙舟的视频门户网站上搜索「玲珑斋说玉」，那上面有我上传的「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」的评测视频，视频里说得比较详细。
t_en: Haha, alright — search "The Iridescent Parlor Talks Jade" on Xianzhou's video portal and you'll find some very detailed videos I've uploaded that review the Auspicious XV.

t_ch: 怎么样，亲亲要不先试用下再做决定吗？
t_en: How about it? Do you wanna try it out first, sweetie?

t_ch: 我还有些问题想问。
t_en: I've still got some questions.

t_ch: 好的亲亲，你还有什么想问的吗？
t_en: Alright babe, what else would you like to ask?

t_ch: 玉兆到底是什么？
t_en: What actually is a jade abacus then?

t_ch: 为了方便化外民理解，亲亲可以认为玉兆是仙舟特有的玉石计算机。
t_en: Darling, to help you understand this as an Outworlder, you can think of a jade abacus as a kind of jade computer unique to Xianzhou.

t_ch: 每一款玉兆都包含着精密而细致的篆刻回路，借助着回路完成复杂的计算，就和你们常见的半导体电路板是一样的。
t_en: Every jade abacus contains a precise and meticulous engraved circuit and uses that to process complex calculations — just like the superconductor circuit boards common in your world.

t_ch: 工造司的「解玉坊」开放「民用玉兆制作标准」之后，各种各样的品牌也像雨后春笋一样，不过我还是最推荐亲亲「瑞兆」。
t_en: After the Jade Decipher Workshop from the Artisanship Commission developed their Standards for Manufacture of Civilian-Purpose Jade Abaci, various brands have sprung onto the market — but I still recommend the Auspicious as the best of the lot.

t_ch: 你还在视频网站上做视频？
t_en: You also uploading videos online?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，现在最时尚的店主都是做视频的，只有那些老古董店主还心存侥幸，盼着顾客路过…
t_en: Haha, all the hottest shopkeepers are making videos these days. Only old antique dealers leave things up to luck, desperate for passers-by to notice them...

t_ch: 再说了，这也算是给自家店面打广告了。观众喜欢我的视频，也会信任我的产品，随后就会到我这里来买玉兆…这就是视频经济。
t_en: Besides, these are adverts for my store. People like my videos, trust my products, and then come into the store to buy a jade abacus... that's how the video economy works.

t_ch: 没想到玉器珠宝会这么潮流…
t_en: I never expected jade jewelry to be so popular...

t_ch: 亲亲，别怪我说话不好听哦。不是「玉器珠宝会这么潮流」，而是你们化外民对玉器珠宝的认识太过古旧了。
t_en: Babe, please forgive me for speaking so frankly. It's not that jade jewelry is so popular here. It's just that Outworlders have an antiquated understanding of jadeware.

t_ch: 在仙舟，这些玉石可不止是漂亮的石头而已。
t_en: Here aboard Xianzhou, these jade items are not just beautiful stones.

t_ch: 感觉你对科技产品过于狂热了…
t_en: I sense that you're a real fiend for tech...

t_ch: 哎，身边很少有人能跟上我的时尚嗅觉，尽是一些抱陈守旧的家伙。我常常感叹这星槎海人来人往，只有我知道「瑞兆」的新算法有多厉害…
t_en: Ah, there aren't many people these days who can keep up with my sense of fashion. People around me are all out-of-touch. I often think that out of the masses of people who passes Starskiff Haven, only I understand how powerful the new Auspicious' algorithm is...

t_ch: 你知道吗，仙舟人虽然所有人看上去都很年轻，但是一旦跟他们聊天，你立刻就能察觉他们全都在发霉了，哎呀，我可受不了。
t_en: You know, everyone aboard Xianzhou looks really young at first glance, but as soon as they open their mouths to speak, you instantly realize just how moldy they are from the inside out. Tsk, I can't stand it.

t_ch: 在仙舟啊，衰老跟年龄没有什么关系，这心态要是老了，人才是真的老了。
t_en: On Xianzhou, aging has nothing to do with your age. If your mind is old, then you truly are old.

t_ch: 我再考虑考虑。
t_en: I'll consider it.

t_ch: 嗯，亲亲多考虑考虑，欢迎随时光临。
t_en: Have a good think about it, sweetie. You're welcome back, any time.

t_ch: 我再考虑考虑。
t_en: I'll consider it.

t_ch: 嗯，亲亲多考虑考虑，欢迎随时光临。
t_en: Have a good think about it, sweetie. You're welcome back, any time.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，这样吧，您只要接入罗浮的网络，搜索「玲珑斋说玉」，那上面有我上传的「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>」的评测影像，那里说得比较详细。
t_en: Hahaha. Alright, just link up with the Luofu internet and search for "The Iridescent Parlor Talks Jade." You'll find some very detailed videos I've uploaded that review the Auspicious XV.

t_ch: 亲亲要是想现在做决定也可以，我这边也准备好了体验用的玉兆。
t_en: If you're ready to make a decision now, darling, I've got a test unit set up here too.

t_ch: 建木竟然长起来了…
t_en: I can't believe the Ambrosial Arbor is growing...

t_ch: 是啊！不过亲亲的消息好像有点滞后呢，建木已经静止下来了哦，这可是官方最新消息。
t_en: Exactly! But I'm afraid that's old news, darling. According to the latest official update, the Ambrosial Arbor has already stopped growing.

t_ch: 亲亲对建木这么感兴趣，是不是想观察建木，又担心离太近了会有危险呢？那亲可要看看咱们这款「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>加」了。
t_en: Dear, you seem very interested in the Ambrosial Arbor. Do you want to observe it, but worried that getting too close might be dangerous? Well, I'd advise that you take a look at our Auspicious XV+, sweetie.

t_ch: 「瑞兆<unbreak>15</unbreak>加」，镜头采用最新的「菜卡微粒子撞击镜头」，越远越清晰，手可触星辰。
t_en: The Auspicious XV+ comes with the latest Reica micro-particle impact lens that can produce clearer images from greater distances and allow you to even easily observe the sky.

t_ch: 怎么样？亲不火速入手一台，在安全距离尽情远观建木盛景吗？
t_en: What do you think, honey? How about buying one and using it to enjoy the marvelous view of the Ambrosial Arbor from a safe distance?

t_ch: 不必了吧…
t_en: I don't think I need it...

t_ch: 好的呢亲，有什么其他需要随时找我聊啊。
t_en: Sure thing, dear. Just talk to me if you need anything else.

t_ch: 想要置换一些宝贝。
t_en: There may be a few things.

t_ch: 这位客人您来得正是时候，赶得早不如赶得巧，今天「赎珠阁」剩下的绝当押货都是些好东西。
t_en: Dear guest, you have arrived just in time. Better in luck than out of it: We have some amazing leftover goods for you.

t_ch: 不瞒您说，注意到刚才离开的客人了吗？我就没告诉他咱这里有什么好东西。
t_en: Let me be frank — see that customer who just left? I didn't mention any of those trinkets to him.

t_ch: 「赎珠阁」区别对待顾客？
t_en: The Jeweler's Pagoda engages in preferential treatment of customers?

t_ch: 客人你有所不知，有些人是奔着做生意来的，小店管这叫顾客。有些人奔着别的目的来，小店也接待不了。
t_en: You may know this, but some come here to do business — I call them customers. Some come with other things in mind. Those I don't entertain.

t_ch: 对顾客，小店都是一视同仁的。对不是顾客的人，那小店也不必用接待客人的方式对待。
t_en: If you're a customer, I treat all of you equally. Otherwise, I am not obligated to treat you as such.

t_ch: 掌柜是不是觉得我特有钱？
t_en: You think I've got deep pockets?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，像客人这样不爱显山露水的人，不是大富大贵，也即将是大富大贵。早点结交认识，以后小店也好做生意嘛。
t_en: Hahaha, for one who shies away from attention, even if you're not wealthy now, you soon will be. I merely have future businesses in mind by befriending you now.

t_ch: 不过，客人还是误会了。刚才那位客人啊，不是来做生意的，所以我也没告诉他咱这里有什么好东西。
t_en: But, you are nonetheless mistaken. That customer just now did not come for business, which is why I did not tell him about my goodies.

t_ch: 你肯定跟每个人都这么说。
t_en: I bet you say this to everyone who walks in.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，客人这么说就见外了。我一向出言谨慎，审词琢句。
t_en: Hahaha, surely, you jest. I'm known for my caution, and weigh my words with great care.

t_ch: 客人可千万不要误会了呀。刚才那位客人啊，不是来做生意的，所以我也没有把好东西留给他。
t_en: Please do not misunderstand. That customer just now did not come for business, which is why I did not tell him about my goodies.

t_ch: 如何识别来者是客，还是另有所图，其实也是有点儿小技巧的。客人要是感兴趣的话，我可以介绍一二…客人要是对典当有兴趣，小店也随时为您服务。
t_en: The power to distinguish between a customer and a troublemaker is a particular trick of this trade. If you are interested, I could reveal a thing or two... I can also be of service if you are interested in our pledges.

t_ch: 细说。
t_en: Do go on.

t_ch: 一般来我们店里的客人有两种，一种是带着东西来的，他们大多会把想要典当的东西小心包装，这是来店里周转济急的。
t_en: There are two types of customers who come in. The first has something on them, and will wrap with great care what they wish to pawn. They come in search of salvation.

t_ch: 另一种客人，他们会关心那些过了典当期限的宝贝，根据自己的喜好挑挑拣拣。
t_en: The other type only care about Curios that have sat on the shelf for too long, and they browse at their fancy.

t_ch: 刚才那位客人，上来就问我「最贵的宝贝是什么」，我心中暗叫不好，暂时缓住了他，等到您朝这边走来，他便扭头走开了。
t_en: That customer just now opened with "what is the most expensive item you have here." It did not sit well with me, after which I delayed him. When you came over, he turned and went away.

t_ch: 您就算不买东西，我也得留着您在这里聊上一会儿呀。
t_en: Even if you aren't buying anything, I have to keep you here for a chat.

t_ch: 原来你把我当避风港了呀？
t_en: So you're using me as an excuse?

t_ch: 刚才那位客人血气方刚，不知道会干出什么。
t_en: That customer just now had a reckless air about him. I did not know what he would've done.

t_ch: 客人愿意帮我，麻烦事在没发生之前就能结束，小店自当感激不尽。
t_en: If you could help conclude a trouble before it started, I would very much appreciate that.

t_ch: 小便宜都让你给占完了。
t_en: I got the short end of the stick.

t_ch: 这也多亏了客人海涵。
t_en: It was all thanks to your kindness.

t_ch: 客人愿意帮我，麻烦事在没发生之前就能结束，小店自当感激不尽。
t_en: If you could help conclude a trouble before it started, I would very much appreciate that.

t_ch: 我现在就立刻离开。
t_en: I'm leaving.

t_ch: 别别别，您就当行行好，再多聊一会儿。我远远见着那人就快失去兴致了。
t_en: Please don't do that. See it as a good deed and stay a while longer. I can see from the corner of my eye that he will quickly lose interest.

t_ch: 客人愿意帮我，麻烦事在没发生之前就能结束，小店自当感激不尽。
t_en: If you could help conclude a trouble before it started, I would very much appreciate that.

t_ch: 我早就看出你的小心思了。
t_en: I already knew what you were trying to pull.

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，一见面我就知道客人慧眼如炬，小店也不便卖弄了。
t_en: Hahaha, I knew at a glance you had a keen eye. I shall refrain from my shenanigans then.

t_ch: 客人看透了前面的客人心怀叵测，还专程来与小掌柜的如此攀谈，江湖救急不言之中，小店真是感激不尽。
t_en: That you notice the sinister intent of the customer and specially approached to chat with me. I will be ever-grateful for your lending hand.

t_ch: 要不这样吧，这是小店的一点儿心意，希望客人能够收下…客人要是对典当有兴趣，小店也随时为您服务。
t_en: How about this: Here's a token of our shop. I hope you will accept it... If you have any interest in pawned items, I shall be fully at your service.

t_ch: 想和掌柜的再聊聊。
t_en: I wish to chat with you a little longer.

t_ch: 客人说这话就客气了，谈生意嘛，先谈才有生意，我也愿意和客人聊聊。
t_en: You're too polite. To conduct business, one must first engage in chat. I am willing to chat with you.

t_ch: 想听一些典当的趣事。
t_en: I wish to hear some amusing stories about this profession.

t_ch: 趣事谈不上，说起来曾经有客人来店里与我打招呼，希望我能把店里珠宝当物的价格尽量往高了抬，我大概理解了他的意图，随即同意了下来。
t_en: I'm not sure if I'd call these events amusing, but there was once a customer who came in and hoped I would raise the prices of the jewelry pieces in the shop. I gathered his motive, and agreed instantly.

t_ch: 过了一会儿，他就带着女伴来店里了。我也尽量给每一枚珠宝当物讲了一个传奇故事，把价格抬到了他根本承担不起的程度。
t_en: After a while, he came in with his lady friend. I tried my best to weave wonderful tales for each and every jewelry piece, and raised their prices beyond what he could afford.

t_ch: 随后，我们便在欢声笑语中完成了交易，皆大欢喜。
t_en: Later, we laughed gaily as we completed the trade, and ended the affair on a happy note.

t_ch: 哪里来的大财主？
t_en: Where did this Mr. Moneybags come from?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，那位客人也不是什么财主，只是好面子而已。
t_en: Hahaha, he is no wealthy man, just a prideful one.

t_ch: 我好像知道发生什么了。
t_en: I think I know what happened.

t_ch: 配合客人的演出而已。
t_en: I was merely playing along with a customer's act.

t_ch: 放心，实际结账的时候，那位客人只支付了原价。
t_en: Don't worry. When the time came to pay, he only had to pay the original price.

t_ch: 典当不是暴利行业吗？
t_en: Is a pawnbroker highly lucrative?

t_ch: 实在谈不上。我这「赎珠阁」开张数百年，就没有遇到什么暴利的机会，全是小本生意，只够勉强营生。
t_en: Not at all. The Jeweler's Pagoda has been open for hundreds of years, but I've never had a chance to make a killing. It's all slow and steady, and I can barely make ends meet.

t_ch: 客人有所不知，在仙舟上古玩的概念总是很模糊，古玩的价值则很含糊。换句话说吧，抛开文化的价值，客人以为仙舟人觉得古玩重要吗？
t_en: You may not know this, but antique collecting on Xianzhou has always been a vague market, as are the prices of antiques. Cultural value aside, do you think that Xianzhou natives consider antiques important?

t_ch: 遗憾的是，大部分仙舟人对一件上百年的瓶瓶罐罐不感兴趣。要是典当客人不觉得古物值钱，那我们也不便高价典当不是？
t_en: What's saddening is that most Xianzhou natives are dismissively uninterested in centuries-old ornaments. If a customer here did not think an antique valuable, it wouldn't be appropriate for us to pawn them at exorbitant prices, would it?

t_ch: 还得多亏了咱们各位化外民老爷，古玩还是古玩，文物还是文物，小店也能赚到一些蝇头小利。
t_en: I have you honored Outworlders to be thankful for — antiques and cultural artifacts all belong where they are, and I can still earn a little off the top.

t_ch: 有什么有趣的典品吗？
t_en: Do you have any interesting collaterals?

t_ch: 有的有的，这位「招财猫尊」快过了当期，无人交当。
t_en: Of course! This Supreme Fortune Cat is about to reach its maturity date, and no one has redeemed it.

t_ch: 古语说「猫洗面过耳则客至」。所以，咱们开当铺的都得准备一位「招财猫尊」刮耳洗面，图的就是生意兴隆的好兆头。
t_en: There's an old saying that "a cat bathing itself heralds a customer's arrival." That is why we must have a Supreme Fortune Cat in our pawnshop — to herald tidings of good fortune.

t_ch: 要是客人对开店经商感兴趣了，可以考虑给自己的小店备一尊。
t_en: If you are interested in opening a shop, you can consider preparing one.

t_ch: 下次再来拜访。
t_en: I'll come back again.

t_ch: 客气了，欢迎客人下次光临惠顾。
t_en: You're too kind. You're welcome back any time.

t_ch: 想要置换一些东西。（打开商店）
t_en: I want to buy some things. (Open Shop)

t_ch: 实在谈不上。我这「赎珠阁」开张数百年，就没有遇到什么暴利的机会，全是小本生意，只够勉强营生。
t_en: Not at all. The Jeweler's Pagoda has been open for hundreds of years, but I've never had a chance to make a killing. It's all slow and steady, and I can barely make ends meet.

t_ch: 所谓的珍玩，除物品「稀有」一事所含的价值外，无非是靠历史、传奇为之增色溢价。
t_en: Apart from the value derived from any inherent rarity, these so-called "treasures" can only raise their worth with relevant histories, tales, or fables.

t_ch: 但对仙舟人而言，化外民所认知的古玩不过是些过时的破烂罢了。谁又会将自己出生时代的旧物当成珍宝呢？
t_en: But to a Xianzhou native, what Outworlders deem ancient artifacts are but expired rubbish. Who would treat items made during their life as treasures?

t_ch: 那为什么不反过来…
t_en: Why not sell the other way round then...

t_ch: …把旧物卖给外面的人？
t_en: ...You mean selling old items to Outworlders?

t_ch: 将「旧物」装成典质品出给化外民？反其道而行之也不是不行。但有二不可——
t_en: Present old items as ancient pieces and sell them to Outworlders? Well, that's possible, I suppose, except for two things:

t_ch: 一则，这些「旧物」遍布仙舟，垂手可得，人家也犯不着从我这儿购入。
t_en: Firstly, Xianzhou's filled with this "old stuff." It's not even hard to find them. People don't have to buy it from me.

t_ch: 二则，如此营生能来一时快钱，但天长日久难免遭拆穿，无异饮鸩止渴。
t_en: Secondly, it may bring in a quick short-term profit, but this scheme would be thwarted by other locals in the long run, and I'd be left with nothing.

t_ch: 所以我想通啦，这爿小店能周转有趣之物，收支平衡就行。
t_en: So, I've made up my mind: I'm satisfied as long as this little shop can peddle in fascinating things and make ends meet.

t_ch: 价值不过是人心中起伏的波澜，我能在这儿看看形形色色的人生，看人心潮起潮落，也就足够了。
t_en: Value is all but determined by fluctuations of the human impulse. How I can meet people from all walks of life and observe the ebb and flow of their hearts is already enough.

t_ch: 下次再来拜访。
t_en: I'll come back again.

t_ch: 客气了，欢迎客人下次光临惠顾。
t_en: You're too kind. You're welcome back any time.

t_ch: 「算盘书字银洋，件件要精，五者缺一，吃亏非小……」
t_en: "Abacuses, books, and silver, all of the finest quality. Taking one without the others would be such a shame..."

t_ch: 客人打算典当还是闲聊，小店也随时为您服务。
t_en: Whether you have something to pawn or just wish to chat, I am always at your service.

t_ch: 客人若是有别的打算，小店也随时为您服务。
t_en: If you have other plans, I am always at your service.

t_ch: 想听一些典当的趣事。
t_en: I wish to hear some amusing stories in your line of work.

t_ch: 说起来，曾有位客人闯进我的店里，言明想要用自己的一只手，来换取三十年寿命。
t_en: Now that we're on the topic, there was a customer who barged into my shop and declared that he wanted to trade his own hand for thirty years of his life.

t_ch: 我笑着问他是不是看了什么讲妖魔当铺的话本故事，误信了小说家言。这爿小店只能以物换钱，或是以钱换物。
t_en: I laughed and asked him if he'd read some nonsensical story about demon pawnshops and actually believed it. This shop only exchanges items for cash, or vice versa.

t_ch: 他自斗篷底下露出自己的右手，让我仔细观瞧。这是一支由活水银铸造的手臂，肌肉纹理细腻如生。他向我演示了这支手臂变化成刀、剑、弓、枪的奇景。
t_en: He then showed his right hand from under his cloak and asked me to scrutinize it. It was an arm forged of living mercury, its muscle contours artistically detailed. Next, he demonstrated the wondrous sight of turning his arm into a saber, a sword, a bow, or a spear.

t_ch: 那位客人受活水银侵蚀，命在旦夕，希望以自己身躯的一部分换来苟延残喘的三十年光阴。不知怎地，他得知有人曾在我这儿典当了一剂逆化灵药。
t_en: This customer had been poisoned by living mercury, and his life was in grave peril. He had hoped to relieve himself of this part of his body in exchange for thirty years of health. He somehow knew that someone had pawned a vial of reversion elixir here.

t_ch: 你答应了吗？
t_en: Did you agree to the deal?

t_ch: 他如愿以偿了？
t_en: Did he get his wish?

t_ch: 我拒绝了。我给了他丹鼎司的地址，医士们或许对这种奇怪绝症感兴趣。
t_en: I declined. I gave him the Alchemy Commission's address. I figured the healers there would be interested in his rare condition.

t_ch: 有什么有趣的当物吗？
t_en: Do you have any interesting pledges?

t_ch: 有位猎人打扮的化外民游客在我这儿典当了一把样式古旧的猎枪。
t_en: A hunter dressed like an outworld traveler pawned an old hunting rifle here once.

t_ch: 他说将这把枪瞄准目标曾驻足的地方，扣动扳机，能跨越光阴一枪击杀猎物。
t_en: He said that if you aim this gun where the target once was and pull the trigger, you could traverse time and kill your target with a single shot.

t_ch: 骗人的吧？
t_en: That's a lie, surely?

t_ch: 怎么可能？
t_en: How's that even possible?

t_ch: 我也不知真假，所以在他走后不久，我心血来潮冲他站的位置放了一枪。
t_en: I couldn't tell if it was true either, so not long after he left, I fired a shot on a whim at where he once stood.

t_ch: 掌柜突然露齿一笑。
t_en: The pawnbroker suddenly breaks out into a toothy grin.

t_ch: 不知道这件物品至今没被赎回，是不是在下的罪过。
t_en: I still wonder if it was my fault that this item hasn't been redeemed.

t_ch: 先前的风波…掌柜能赚不少吧？
t_en: You earned a great fortune from the previous crisis, didn't you?

t_ch: 客人您说笑了，要是一般的危机，在商言商，那的确是个低买高卖的好时间，何况仙舟上最不缺的便是时间……
t_en: You must be teasing me. If the crisis had been an ordinary one, it would indeed have been a perfect time to buy low and sell high. We all have plenty of time on Xianzhou to wait for prices to rise, after all...

t_ch: 可这建木之事非同凡响，闹得人心惶惶的，咱们当铺也不例外，我也是土生土长的仙舟人嘛，这阵仗还是第一次见。我甚至一度考虑要不要关门大吉窝家里躲着去。
t_en: But the Ambrosial Arbor is no ordinary crisis. It put everyone on edge, and my pawn shop was no exception. Even as someone born and raised on Xianzhou, it was the first time I have seen something as serious as this. I even considered closing my shop to hide at home.

t_ch: 好在这事总算过去了，建木不再生长，咱们的生活也重回正常。至于客官您这话，我就当是您给咱们小店的诚挚祝福吧。
t_en: To everyone's relief, the crisis is now over. The Ambrosial Arbor has stopped growing, allowing our lives to get back to normal. As for your words, I will take them as your sincere blessing to my shop.

t_ch: 曲木达直终必弯，夸谈戏言求真难。假作真时真亦假，听我说书得交钱。
t_en: Curved wood must be bent again to reach straightness, and finding truth from exaggerations and lies takes knack. When the real is fake, the fake is also real, and you need to toss a coin to hear my spiel.

t_ch: 在下是这「不夜侯」的说书人西衍。这位客官要是想听点儿成本大套的长篇，你可得在这儿等一会儿了。
t_en: I am Xiyan, a humble storyteller at the Sleepless Earl. If you'd like to listen to a few novel-length tales, you might have to wait here for a while.

t_ch: …话又说回来，虽然说书场时间未到，但要是能买点儿茶饮，听上一会儿小曲儿，也不算浪费这须臾功夫，您看如何呀？
t_en: ...Then again, though there is still time for the story sessions, if you were to sip some tea and listen to a few tunes, it wouldn't be a complete waste of time, would it?

t_ch: 你这说书人还管茶楼生意？
t_en: You are a storyteller, but also involved with running the tea house here?

t_ch: 这位客官，您看你这话说的。这茶楼生意好，来喝茶的客官就多，这喝茶的客官多了，我这也能观众老爷们套套交情，赚点儿小钱嘛。
t_en: {M#Good sir}{F#Madam}, don't talk like that. If the tea house has business, then more patrons will come to drink tea. With many patrons, I then can make friends and earn a little money for myself.

t_ch: 再说了，我看这「不夜侯」的茶品不错，您要是愿意听在下推荐，点上一壶「狩原毛峰」尝尝滋味，保准满意。
t_en: Besides, the tea at the Sleepless Earl isn't too shabby. If you'd like some recommendations, I'd say order a pot of Hunter Maofeng tea. Satisfaction guaranteed.

t_ch: 好呀，正打算点些茶水。
t_en: Great. I was just about to order some tea.

t_ch: 那您就来对地方了。这「不夜侯」是罗浮仙舟首屈一指的品茗胜地。别看老板娘年纪轻轻，在「铫煎黄蕊」上的研究可真是无人能出其右。
t_en: Then you've come to the right place. The Sleepless Earl is the premier tea spot aboard the Xianzhou Luofu. Don't let the innkeeper's young age fool you. Her research on how to simmer tea leaves is unrivaled.

t_ch: 您要是愿意听在下推荐，点上一壶「狩原毛峰」尝尝滋味，保准满意。
t_en: If you wouldn't mind my humble opinion, order a pot of Hunter Maofeng tea. Satisfaction guaranteed.

t_ch: 有一些懂得茶道的客人经常来「不夜侯」高谈阔论，在下虽然不才，也偷听到了一些门道，可以给您卖弄一下。您看怎样？
t_en: Some guests are tea aficionados, and often pontificate about all kinds of expert tea lore at the Sleepless Earl. I'm not a smart man, but I have memorized some facts and can show them off. Would you be interested?

t_ch: 别光说这茶了，你说说书吧。
t_en: Don't just tell me about tea. Tell me one of your stories.

t_ch: …这可为难在下呀。说书的没有什么本事，只准备了几个本子，要是都讲完了，接下来就只能喝西北风了。
t_en: ... You put me in a difficult spot. Storytellers don't have much except a few books stored in our heads. I'll become a pauper if I ever told them all.

t_ch: 客官您要是愿意听点儿闲言碎语，我倒是可以给您讲讲。
t_en: If you're willing to listen to a bit of gossip, then I can certainly fill you in.

t_ch: 那你再给我讲讲茶吧。
t_en: Tell me more about tea, then.

t_ch: 那我讲讲这「鳞渊春」吧。「钟鳞渊之灵禀，擅飞云之秀气」，一品茗香，卧看星海。
t_en: Let me tell you about the Whale-Tide Spring tea, then. They say "one cup of Whale-Tide Spring invigorates the sense and brings the beauty of clouds." Lay back, imbibe, and watch the starry seas.

t_ch: 据传持明龙尊引得古海潮动如雷，淹覆鳞渊境洞天。又数十年余，持明后裔在一座浮岛上找到了一株盛放茶树，标枝秀立，芽叶如玉。
t_en: It's said that the Vidyadhara high elder roiled the ancient seas like thunder, and submerged the Scalegorge Waterscape delve. Dozens of years later, the Vidyadhara descendants found a flourishing tea tree on a floating isle — its sturdy branches were beautiful, and its leaves lush like jade.

t_ch: 哎呀，一想到这亦真亦假的造化故事，茶汤就仿佛多了一道韵味，您不会这么觉得吗？
t_en: Ahh... thinking over such half-truths and legends really lends the tea some extra texture. Don't you think?

t_ch: 想听听仙舟的传奇故事。
t_en: I want to hear about the legends of Xianzhou.

t_ch: 在下可以讲一下被称为「无罅飞光」云骑剑首…但奈何篇幅过长，仅是讲讲苍城仙舟被游星罗睺摧毁之事，便要上三天三夜。
t_en: I could tell you of the Cloud Knight Sword Champion known as the Transcendent Flash... but it's far too long. Just the chapter about the Roaming Star Rahu destroying the Xianzhou Cangcheng takes three whole days and three whole nights.

t_ch: 仙舟人自是毫不介意…在下看客官是化外之民，不好虚掷光阴，这里就不展开谈了。
t_en: The Xianzhou natives don't mind this story at all... but seeing as you are an Outworlder, I don't want to waste your precious time — so I will spare you from it.

t_ch: 啊不过…您要是赏脸来赶上说书场，那就权当另说。
t_en: Ahhh, but... if you would flatter me by coming to my show, then that's another matter entirely.

t_ch: 想知道你和老板娘的关系。
t_en: Tell me more about your connection to the owner here.

t_ch: 嗨，客官目光敏锐，实在瞒不住您。
t_en: Well well well! You have a keen eye. I can't hide that from you.

t_ch: 这小老板娘的父母与在下是旧识，他们将这一方小小茶楼留于小老板娘当作考验。
t_en: Her parents are old friends of mine, and they left her this teahouse here as a kind of a test.

t_ch: 狐人商家的本事大多是这样历练出来的。在下受小老板娘父母之托，来这里帮衬帮衬她的生意，暗中指导一二。
t_en: Most foxian traders learn their skills this way. I was asked by her parents to help her run the business from behind the scenes, offering a little guidance or two.

t_ch: 不过小老板娘天资聪颖，想必很快就能独当一面了。
t_en: But she is incredibly talented and intelligent. Soon enough, she'll be able to take charge of everything by herself.

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 行，等客官什么时候方便，我们再来聊聊。
t_en: Okay, let's chat again when you have the time.

t_ch: 怎么仙舟上还有说书人呀？
t_en: There are storytellers aboard Xianzhou?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，经常有化外民问起这个问题——「你们仙舟上科技水平还挺高的，怎么还有人说书呀？」
t_en: Hahaha, I hear this from Outworlders all the time: "Xianzhou is so technologically advanced. Why do you still have storytellers?"

t_ch: 我明白这话外之意，无非就是在说「仙舟上娱乐方式那么多，怎么还在搞这老一套的东西？」，甚至还有人觉得我特别俗气，不应该出现在星槎海这里。
t_en: I know what the question's really asking though. They want to say "there's so much to do on Xianzhou, yet there's something as old-fashioned as this?" Some even consider my presence tacky, that I'm out of place on Starskiff Haven.

t_ch: 说书啊，确实是一门没落的艺术，后继无人算是一个问题，没有地方登台表演问题更为严重——无论是说书、相声，这都是要面对观众的手艺。
t_en: Storytelling is indeed a declining art. Few wishes to carry on this tradition, which is a problem. Far more serious is the lack of a stage — be it storytelling or crosstalk, these are art forms that require an audience.

t_ch: 我认识一些靠卖艺维生的曲艺人，戏台子早就没了，想唱戏也没地方…一门艺术就这样消亡了，我还挺舍不得的。
t_en: I know of artistes who rely on their performance for a living. The theater is a thing of the past, and there's nowhere to sing opera... An art form meeting its demise just like that... a true pity.

t_ch: 所以你看我，一个说书人管什么茶水生意啊？要不是这小老板娘提供这块场地、给咱赏饭吃，我们这些老艺术家也没地儿吃饭了。
t_en: And so you see me and ask: What's a storyteller doing, manning a teahouse? If it weren't for the little owner providing me with a venue and food, us old artistes wouldn't be able to feed ourselves.

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 行，等客官什么时候方便，我们再来聊聊。
t_en: Okay, let's chat again when you have the time.

t_ch: 势利交怀势利心，斯文谁复念知音。台上说书台下友，结缘便值万两金。
t_en: Snobs they come and snobs they go, and good friends and manners are hard to come by. A storyteller on stage and a friend below, an acquaintance is worth more than thousands of gold.

t_ch: 话这位客官您还是来得不巧，节目可能还需要等等。但是客官要想听我论茶的话，我还能小小地卖弄一下。
t_en: Unfortunately, you're still far too early for the show. But stick around if you don't mind listening to me chat about tea.

t_ch: 给我讲一段「云上五骁」吧。
t_en: Tell me something from the High-Cloud Quintet.

t_ch: 既然是客人要求，那我就讲一段「饮月君」的故事。
t_en: If the customer wishes for it, then allow me to regale you with a tale from the Imbibitor Lunae.

t_ch: 咱们得先讲到幽囚狱，这一天，关押了一名异星来客，因为当街击杀了七八名云骑士兵，作为重犯被押至牢狱深处。
t_en: We must first speak of the Shackling Prison. On this day, a visitor from another star was jailed for slaying seven or eight Cloud Knights Soldiers, and was thrown into the depths of the prison for this heinous crime.

t_ch: 这囚徒体格惊人，身高过丈，腰大十围，独大体胖，方面大耳，天生金甲覆身，刀枪不入…为了抓它，十王司还派了几名判官协同抓捕。
t_en: This prisoner's physique was shocking, standing over ten feet tall with a girth of over five feet. Everything was huge about him: His weight, visage, and facial features. His body was covered in natural golden armor, and impervious to weapons... To capture him, the Ten-Lords Commission had to dispatch its Judges to assist.

t_ch: 「这里就是牢狱最深处？」这铁皮囚犯声音低沉，就好像是观光游客向当地人打听景点信息，丝毫没有锒铛入狱的荒颓样儿。
t_en: "Is this the lowest depths of the prison?" This iron-skinned prisoner's voice was deep, his tone akin to a sightseer asking about the scenery. There was not a hint of desperation from one just thrown in prison.

t_ch: 「闭嘴！」一旁负责押送的云骑军对这铁皮囚犯十分不满，他刚才在抓捕行动里折了两根肋骨，现在还隐隐作痛。
t_en: "Shut up!" A Cloud Knight transporting him barked, obviously upset. He had had two ribs broken during the capture operation, and could still feel the throbbing pain.

t_ch: 「这里就是牢狱最深处？」这铁皮囚犯声音更加低沉地再问了一遍。这一问不打紧，一位在阴影之中饮茶的青年注意到了这名囚犯。
t_en: "Is this the lowest depths of the prison?" This iron-skinned prisoner asked again, his voice lowered another notch. With this question, a youth sipping tea in the shadows had noticed this prisoner.

t_ch: 饮月君！
t_en: Imbibitor Lunae!

t_ch: （太长了，下次再听吧）
t_en: (This story is taking too long. I'll listen to it another time.)

t_ch: 「…算了，再问你们也没有用，不如我自己下去探探。」这铁皮囚犯话刚说完，两名云骑军就像是被一双有力的大手捏住了喉咙，顿时昏了过去。
t_en: "...Forget it. It's pointless asking you a lot. I'll just have to explore down below myself." Just as this iron-skinned prisoner finished his sentence, the two Cloud Knights were instantly knocked out, their throats seemingly strangled by a pair of powerful giant hands.

t_ch: 定睛一看，云骑军的覆面铁甲已经完全变形。这异星而来的铁皮囚犯能够用意念控制金属，这两名云骑军根本不是他的对手。于是他再次向青年发问——
t_en: Upon closer observation, the iron visors that the Cloud Knights wore were completely mangled. This iron-skinned prisoner from another star could use his will to control metal, and these two Knights were definitely not his match. And so, he once again asked the youth —

t_ch: 「这里就是牢狱最深处？」
t_en: "Is this the lowest depths of the prison?"

t_ch: 饮茶人背后的手铐、脚镣、脖锁、刑枷各种刑具开始铮铮作响，铁皮囚犯已经用他的御铁之术包围了饮茶人。
t_en: The myriad of cuffs, shackles, chains, and spikes hanging behind the tea drinker began to rattle, for the iron-skinned prisoner had already used his iron-wielding skill to entrap the youth.

t_ch: 而这位饮茶人，不慌不忙地再倒了一杯茶，并将茶杯举起，像是在邀请铁皮囚犯一般，他的声音清冷，但又引人生畏——
t_en: Yet this tea drinker poured himself another cup without missing a beat, then raised his cup, as if inviting the iron-skinned prisoner over. His voice was clear and cold, yet intimidating...

t_ch: 「看来你对控制金属颇为自信，我只用一杯茶水如何？」
t_en: "It appears you're rather confident of your metal manipulation. How about I just use one cup of tea?"

t_ch: 好家伙！云吟法术。
t_en: My goodness! Cloudcry magic.

t_ch: （太长了，下次再听吧）
t_en: (This story is taking too long. I'll listen to it another time.)
